results.title: The Spy who loved me (Special edition)
results.creator_credit: Lewis Gilbert, Albert R. Broccoli, United Artists Corporation and Ken Adam
results.brief_description: The Spy who loved me was the third performance by Roger Moore as James Bond, British secret service agent 007. In this instalment Bond is sent to foil Karl Stromberg - a megalomaniac villain who steals the plans to a nuclear tracking device that will allow him to control missiles and rule the world. Bond is sent to Cairo and teams with sexy Russian agent Anya Amasova (Barbara Bach), fending off seductive women and murderous men, in an attempt to retrieve the microfilm. Famous sequences include the pre-credits ski hill chase and a thrilling pursuit through Cairo that turns into an underwater adventure thanks to some deft British technology. Stromberg’s underwater headquarters make an impressive set piece, and provides ample opportunities for him to feed enemies to man-eating sharks. Richard Kiel stars as Jaws - a memorably gruesome Bond enemy with menacing metal choppers for weaponry. This Bond is replete with impressive stunts, action sequences and wisecracks from the wry lady’s man, but is less campy than its predecessors, “Live and let die” and “The Man with the golden gun”, both of which starred Moore.

results.title: The Rutles
results.creator_credit: Eric Idle, Gary Weis, Craig Kellem and Rutles
results.brief_description: The Rutles is a mockumentary, a parody of The Beatles and Beatlemania written and co-directed by Eric Idle, of Monty Python fame. The Rutles are Dirk, Barry, Stig and Nasty. Made for television, “The Rutles” charts the rise of the band of the same name as they progress from English obscurity to world phenomena. Using mockumentary footage and interviews, “The Rutles” documents the pre-fab four’s journey right through to their messy break up and post-Rutle lives. Idle plays a series of characters including Rutle Dirk and a hapless BBC type interviewer who narrates the film. “The Rutles” mocks the BBC, documentary filmmakers, and The Beatles’ films and music. Features excerpts from The Rutles films - Yellow Submarine Sandwich, Tragical History Tour, OUCH!, and A Hard Day’s Rut. Songs include Please rut me, All you need is cash and Twist and rut. “The Rutles” also features George Harrison, John Belushi, Dan Ackroyd, Bianca Jagger, Ron Wood, Mick Jagger, Paul Simon and Bill Murray. “The Rutles” is a sharp and witty send up of any number of documentaries made about The Beatles.

results.title: The Rescuers
results.creator_credit: Art Stevens, John Lounsbery, Wolfgang Reitherman and Walt Disney Productions
results.brief_description: Courageous rescues are the specialty of secret agent mice, Bernard and Bianca. An innocent orphan named Penny is kidnapped by Madame Medusa, a wickedly wacky villainess. Madame Medusa needs Penny to recover a priceless jewel. Bernard and Bianca set out to save Penny from Madame Medusa’s evil clutches. Voices are provided by Bob Newhart, Eva Gabor and Geraldine Page. Animated.

results.title: The Prince of Egypt
results.creator_credit: Brenda Chapman, Simon Wells, Steve Hickner, Penney Finkelman Cox, Sandra Rabins and NBCUniversal
results.brief_description: An animated musical film which tells the story of Moses, based on the book of Exodus in the Old Testament. Follows Moses from the moment his mother sends him out onto the River Nile in a basket of reeds, to his triumphant leading of his people out of slavery in Egypt. In “Prince of Egypt” Dreamworks Pictures attempts to make an animated film on the scale of an epic film. It combines the cute cornball of animated characters and musical numbers with lavish biblical backdrops, such as the magnificently realised parting of the red sea. The cast of voices includes a who’s who of Hollywood stars. (Awards: Winner of 1998 Academy Award for Best Original Song - “When You Believe”)

results.title: The Pagemaster
results.creator_credit: Joe Johnston, Pixote Hunt, David Kirschner, Paul Gertz, Turner Pictures and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Richard is a shy boy but one day when he seeks refuge in a public library during a storm, he is suddenly transported into an incredible animated world filled with adventure and fantasy. In this world, anything is possible. The only limits are set by the imagination. Some of the greatest stories are made part of Richard’s life in the mystical world of “The Pagemaster.” Stars Macaulay Culkin as Richard Tyler, Christopher Lloyd as The Pagemaster, Ed Begley Jnr as Richard’s father and the voices of Whoopi Goldberg, Patrick Stewart, Frank Welker and Leonard Nimoy.

results.title: The Little Drummer Girl
results.creator_credit: George Roy Hill, Robert L. Crawford and Warner Bros Pictures
results.brief_description: Diane Keaton stars as Charlie, an American actress and Palestinian sympathiser. Klaus Kinski is the head of an Israeli counterspy group who sees Charlie’s romantic vulnerability as the perfect trap for the elusive Palestinian terrorist Khalil. Based on the novel by John Le Carre.

results.title: The Litterbug
results.creator_credit: Hamilton Luske and Walt Disney Productions
results.brief_description: Modern science continues to wage its war on pests of all type. But even science makes little progress against that contemptible pest - the litterbug. Who but Donald Duck should be better suited to portray this scourge of the cities beaches and mountains. With great skill, daring and deep dedication to his task, he methodically deposits his litter for all to see, happy in the realisation that no likely spot has been overlooked.

results.title: The Life of mammals: by David Attenborough. Ep.1-10
results.creator_credit: Mike Salisbury, BBC and Discovery Channel
results.brief_description: A comprehensive four disc set of the complete television series written and presented by Sir David Attenborough on the life of mammals. A moving orchestral score and majestic slow-motion wildlife cinematography is combined to great effect with the dulcet tones of Sir David making this another winning combination from the BBC/Discovery Channel co-production team. All ten episodes that make up the series run for approximately 50 minutes each. Music by Dan Jones and Ben Salisbury. Follow up website at www.bbc.co.uk/nature. Episode producers are: Mike Salisbury, Mark Lindfield, Vanessa Berlowitz, Neil Lucas and Huw Cordey. The episodes are: Ep.1 “A Winning design”. Evolutionary finesse has gone someway to making the mammal an effective survivor. Ep.2 “Insect hunters” looks at the way some animals need to consume insects. Ep.3 “Plant predators” shows the call for for some mammals to vigorously consume plants. Ep. 4 “Chisellers” looks at mammals with sharp front teeth - rodents from around the world. Ep. 5 “Meat eaters” shows the carnivorous nature of some mammals - not as a choice but a case of survival. Ep. 6 “Opportunists” is about the adaptable omnivore’s successful food choices. Ep. 7 “Return to water” shows the small number of mammals that through evolutionary necessity have returned to the water. Ep. 8 “Life in the trees” looks at tree dwelling mammals and their adaptation to that particular environment. Ep. 9 “Social climbers” looks at the community and group nature of some types of mammals. Ep. 10 “Food for thought” is a contemplation on the human as the most successful mammal of them all.

results.title: The Golden Louis
results.creator_credit: D.W. Griffith and American Mutoscope and Biograph Company
results.brief_description: A tale of choice between a gambler’s need and his pity for a young homeless girl. Cast includes Anita Hendrie, Adele DeGarde, Owen Moore, Charles Inslee, Mack Sennett. Silent film with English intertitles.

results.title: The Firm Man
results.brief_description: Businessman Gerald Barker is invited to join a mysterious super corporation known as The Firm. His work in the organisation turns out to be of a unique and somewhat disturbing nature. But Gerald finds no comfort with his wife Melissa and friend Barry - his relationships with both collapse. In a surreal and strange fashion, Gerald eventually comes to understand what The Firm is about. Made on a tiny budget of $15,000 the film is a mix of naturalism and stylisation which the filmmaker hoped would work on ‘a simple, surrealistic level’. An early film from the director of “The Year My Voice Broke”, “Mouth to Mouth” and “Far East”.
